item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

I feel like there's a good core story here and I like the physical depiction and concept, but it feels like you've made a lot of incorrect assumptions that readers will be keeping up with you, resulting in a lot of basic gaps in the telling between big, swinging story moments. Much like Croquembouche says. For example, in the logs I think I as able to piece together what was going on in terms of people's locations and actions, but you omitted a lot of basic description and included some things that just made it more confusing. Ditto the line between conceptual/noosphere and reality, I never really got what was where.

A small example of a seeming disconnect: "Subjects with knowledge of SCP-5974 who have not seen the entity…"…. isn't that us, reading this file, as well as all the people discussing it in the meetings and logs?

Another example was you describing Reynes in plain terms as real, then saying that the entity looks like Reynes, and then saying they never existed. Again, I think i see what you were going for, but that kind of retrocausal fuckery is something that you have to build up and pay off, whereas here it just feels like you're saying at least three contradictory facts in the same "voice."

There's leaving room for mystery and then there's just being vague, and this is the latter. I believe you want me to be scratching my head about the Azrael connection, but I'm scratching my head about basic story elements and concepts.

I'm only downvoting on the hopes that you'll fix it. It feels like this is something you could iron out with another pass or two through it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Croq said pretty much everything I was thinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The initial description of this is clunky. The size of the rings were never specified, so I started out assuming they were the size of finger-rings. The amount of visual detail, while appreciated, was almost overwhelming without any context for it. Then "or other concepts associated with Abrahamic faiths" really threw me, because so far there's no indication that this is religious at all. I worked it out eventually, but without the necessary background knowledge, I would've been totally lost.

Once I knew that this article is a reference to something else and not something original, the next few paragraphs lost their significance. Each of them throws a new anomalous effect at me, and most of them are compulsion effects - plus the time-altering stuff that happens later in the article. There's just too much going on.

I found it difficult to understand the context of much of the logs, what the team's goals were, or what actually happened. I'm also finding myself lost on what was at stake here, or what the significance of anything was, or why the containment process is so convoluted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think my favorite part of this is the summoning of Azrael to collect their wayward sibling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a fan at first, when I read the description I just thought it was some low-effort attempt to make a bible-acurate angel an SCP. later on it got cooler with the things about the experiments and the elements. I can see the ammount of effort you put into this, and even tho is not a 100% original idea, it still is a very cool way to develop it. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very well put-together but I'm feeling like… mm, it's hard to describe. The conceptual/noosphere aspect seems thematically detached from the anomaly itself and I have a little trouble picturing what's going on in the logs? Additionally I'm not really getting an idea of how much threat this poses, maybe a mock news headline could be included in the addenda or something.

The ending is likewise rather odd, dropping a lot of info without much buildup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apart from the whole bible-accurate angel thing, nothing about this really interested or engaged me. Maybe I'm just tired of religious SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is this an attempt to make a Bible-accurate angel an SCP?

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
First
El critters
Doctor Fullham Eads Nostson
